metadata.scope: Open-weight LLM deployment sizing on Huawei Ascend AI hardware, using public data and conservative engineering assumptions.

hardware.name: Ascend 910B
hardware.notes: Public 910B references differ on exact HBM capacity. This planner uses a 64GB planning assumption and should be reconciled with the actually purchasable Huawei Cloud SKU.

hardware.name: Ascend 950DT
hardware.notes: 950DT is treated as a roadmap estimate based on public 2025-2026 reporting, not as a confirmed LA-Santiago cloud SKU.

models.name: GLM-5.1
models.notes: GLM-5.1 is public on zai-org/GLM-5.1 with MIT license. The 744B total / 40B active figures come from Z.ai ecosystem sources; Hugging Face currently reports model-size metadata around 754B. The KV cache factor is a conservative planning assumption for DSA-style long-context deployment and must be validated by framework benchmarks.

quantizations.name: BF16
quantizations.rationale: Highest-quality baseline, but the full-weight memory footprint is extremely large.

quantizations.name: FP8
quantizations.rationale: Public model card and provider pages list BF16/FP8 deployment; FP8 is the practical quality-first production baseline.

quantizations.name: NVFP4 / MXFP4
quantizations.rationale: Public quantized derivatives include NVIDIA/AMD 4-bit formats; use when memory budget matters but engineering quality remains important.

quantizations.name: AWQ 4-bit
quantizations.rationale: Community AWQ 4-bit derivatives are visible on Hugging Face and are useful for lower-memory trials.

quantizations.name: GGUF IQ2/2.7bpw
quantizations.rationale: Very low-bit GGUF ports reduce memory sharply, but quality loss is material; treat as experimentation rather than production coding.

models.name: DeepSeek-V4-Flash
models.notes: DeepSeek's model card lists V4-Flash at 284B total / 13B active with 1M context. The official instruct checkpoint uses mixed FP4 expert weights plus FP8 for most other weights; the sizing model uses bit-width equivalents for planning.

quantizations.name: FP8 Mixed Base
quantizations.rationale: The base checkpoint is listed as FP8 Mixed; choose this for conservative quality and simpler serving.

quantizations.name: FP4 + FP8 Mixed
quantizations.rationale: The official V4-Flash instruct checkpoint uses FP4 experts plus FP8 for most other weights, making it the most relevant local-deployment baseline.

quantizations.name: 4-bit MLX
quantizations.rationale: Community 4-bit MLX ports exist for Apple Silicon style deployments; use as a lower-memory reference rather than an Ascend production format.

models.name: GLM-4.5
models.notes: A practical Z.ai baseline from the previous model generation.

quantizations.name: FP8
quantizations.rationale: Quality-first deployment for coding, reasoning, and serious business Q&A.

quantizations.name: INT8
quantizations.rationale: Balanced quality and throughput for production inference.

quantizations.name: INT4 / AWQ
quantizations.rationale: Roughly halves weight memory and is useful when card count is constrained.

models.name: Qwen3-Coder-480B-A35B
models.notes: Native 256K context with YaRN extension to 1M. Long context makes KV cache the main bottleneck.

quantizations.name: FP8
quantizations.rationale: Official FP8 is the quality-first coding option.

quantizations.name: FP4
quantizations.rationale: A better fit for long-context or high-concurrency memory pressure.

quantizations.name: AWQ 4-bit
quantizations.rationale: Community AWQ quantization for availability-first low-memory trials.

models.name: DeepSeek-R1
models.notes: Strong reasoning model, but the full checkpoint is large. Smaller clusters should also evaluate Distill variants.

quantizations.name: FP8
quantizations.rationale: Prioritizes reasoning quality.

quantizations.name: INT8
quantizations.rationale: Balanced production-quality baseline.

quantizations.name: INT4
quantizations.rationale: Feasible for memory-constrained deployments, but complex reasoning quality needs regression testing.

models.name: Llama 3.3 70B Instruct
models.notes: Smaller, mature ecosystem model and a safe baseline for small 910B pilots.

quantizations.name: BF16
quantizations.rationale: Highest quality for low-concurrency services.

quantizations.name: INT8
quantizations.rationale: Balanced quality and performance.

quantizations.name: INT4 / AWQ
quantizations.rationale: Makes long context and multi-user testing easier on two 910B-class cards.

models.name: Qwen3-235B-A22B
models.notes: Apache 2.0 model suitable for general bilingual chat, RAG, and limited coding tasks.

quantizations.name: FP8
quantizations.rationale: Quality-first option.

quantizations.name: INT8
quantizations.rationale: Balanced production baseline.

quantizations.name: INT4
quantizations.rationale: Useful for two-to-four-card pilots.

cloudResources.recommendation: ECS s3.medium.2 was selected in LA-Santiago
cloudResources.reason: Terraform queried the region and selected the smallest available on-demand flavor for static Nginx hosting. This ECS hosts the website only; it is not the inference cluster.
Enough for Ubuntu, Nginx, static assets, and logs. Add a data disk only if collection or audit history grows.
Sufficient for a public demo and sizing tool. Add ELB/CDN and HTTPS for production.

cloudResources.recommendation: Not created by default; optional RDS MySQL 8.0
cloudResources.reason: The current site is static and ships its public dataset with the code. Create RDS only when saved scenarios, accounts, or audit history are required.
HTTP/HTTPS are public. SSH should be restricted to an office or VPN CIDR before production use.
The module lives in terraform/ai-hardware-config-site and uses temporary local tfvars for credentials.

sources.title: Huawei Cloud LA-Santiago region code
sources.summary: Huawei Cloud documentation lists LA-Santiago with region code la-south-2.

sources.title: Ascend 910B public hardware specs
sources.summary: Public hardware references provide planning-level HBM and compute figures, though exact SKU memory can vary.

sources.title: Ascend 950DT roadmap
sources.summary: Public reporting mentions 144GB HBM and high-bandwidth plans for Ascend 950DT. Treat as roadmap, not a confirmed cloud SKU.

sources.title: GLM-5.1 Hugging Face model card
sources.summary: Z.ai's GLM-5.1 model card lists MIT license, local serving options, and coding/agentic benchmark positioning.

sources.title: GLM-5.1 Z.ai docs
sources.summary: Z.ai docs list GLM-5.1 as a flagship foundation model with 200K context and 128K maximum output tokens.

sources.title: GLM-5.1 quantized derivatives
sources.summary: Hugging Face lists multiple quantized derivatives for zai-org/GLM-5.1, including GGUF, AWQ 4-bit, FP8, NVFP4, MXFP4, and MLX variants.

sources.title: DeepSeek-V4-Flash model card
sources.summary: The deepseek-ai model card lists V4-Flash at 284B total parameters, 13B active parameters, 1M context, MIT license, and FP4+FP8 mixed precision for the instruct model.

sources.title: DeepSeek-V4-Flash config
sources.summary: The model config exposes max_position_embeddings 1048576, 43 layers, one KV head, FP4 expert dtype, and FP8 quantization metadata.

sources.title: Qwen3-Coder-480B-A35B model card
sources.summary: The model card lists 480B total / 35B active parameters and long-context deployment details.

sources.title: DeepSeek-R1 model card
sources.summary: The model card describes DeepSeek-R1 open weights and related distilled dense models under the MIT license.

sources.title: Llama 3.3 70B context
sources.summary: Public partner documentation lists Llama 3.3 70B Instruct with a 128K context window.
